summary: Irish-born British novelist and philosopher. Known for Under the Net (Jake Donaghue, dog Mr. Mars), The Sea, The Sea (Charles Arrowby, Booker Prize), The Bell, The Black Prince, A Severed Head. Suffered from Alzheimer's; married to John Bayley.

works.name: Under the Net
works.description: First novel. Jake Donaghue kidnaps movie star dog Mr. Mars; adapts conversations with Hugo Belfounder into The Silencer.

clues.clue: Jake Donaghue kidnaps movie star dog Mr. Mars with Finn O'Finney
examples: Jake Donaghue decides to steal a film star dog named Mister Mars

clues.clue: Jake adapts conversations with Hugo Belfounder into The Silencer
examples: adapts his conversations with Hugo Bellfounder into the book The Silencer

clues.clue: Jake searches for ex Anna Quentin in Paris on Bastille Day
examples: searches for his ex-girlfriend Anna Quentin in Paris on Bastille Day

works.name: The Sea, The Sea
works.description: Booker-winning novel about retired playwright Charles Arrowby obsessing over first love Mary Hartley Fitch at Shruff End.

clues.clue: Charles Arrowby — retired playwright/director; obsesses over Mary Hartley Fitch at Shruff End
examples: former dramatist obsesses over Mary Hartley Fitch

clues.clue: Titus drowns; Arrowby blames husband of childhood crush
examples: blames the young Titus' drowning on the husband

clues.clue: Saved from whirlpool by levitating Buddhist cousin; sees dragon on LSD
examples: saved from a whirlpool by his levitating Buddhist cousin

works.name: Other Novels
works.description: The Bell, The Black Prince, A Severed Head, etc.

clues.clue: The Bell — Imber Court; Catherine Fawley driven mad; Toby Gashe finds bell in lake
examples: Toby Gashe discovers the title object at the bottom of a lake

clues.clue: The Black Prince — Bradley Pearson convicted of Arnold Baffin's murder; 'Post-Scripts' by dramatis personae
examples: Bradley Pearson is convicted for the murder of Arnold Baffin

clues.clue: A Severed Head — Palmer Anderson steals Martin Lynch-Gibbon's wife Antonia
examples: Palmer Anderson tries to steal Antonia

works.name: General
works.description: Alzheimer's; married to John Bayley; philosopher.

clues.clue: Suffered from Alzheimer's; John Bayley wrote Elegy for Iris; Jackson's Dilemma showed vocabulary decline
examples: A book titled 'Elegy for' this author chronicled her struggle with Alzheimer's
had a lifelong affair with Philippa Foot

clues.clue: Philosopher — wrote The Sovereignty of Good, reviving Platonism
examples: The Sovereignty of Good, which revived Platonism in analytic moral philosophy

comprehensive_summary: Iris Murdoch is an Irish-born British novelist and philosopher. Under the Net follows Jake Donaghue kidnapping the dog Mr. Mars. The Sea, The Sea won the Booker Prize and features Charles Arrowby. The Bell is set at Imber Court. The Black Prince follows Bradley Pearson. She suffered from Alzheimer's; John Bayley wrote Elegy for Iris.

cards.front: Novel: Jake Donaghue kidnaps movie star dog Mr. Mars
Novel: adapts conversations with Hugo Belfounder into The Silencer
Novel: retired playwright Charles Arrowby obsesses over his first love Mary Hartley Fitch at coastal Shruff End; Booker Prize winner
Novel: Toby Gashe finds a bell at the bottom of a lake near Imber Abbey
Novel: prospective nun Catherine Fawley driven mad
Novel: Bradley Pearson convicted of Arnold Baffin's murder
Novel: narrated with 'Post-Scripts' by dramatis personae
Author: husband John Bayley wrote Elegy for Iris
Author: last novel Jackson's Dilemma showed vocabulary decline
